title: Sample Prep with DMSO and CSV Input
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol preps up to two 96 well plates with reagent and sample. The protocol takes a csv as input to determine which wells in the middle rack (see below) will be populated. The csv must include the header, with the following order in the header: `vial_id, parent_well, dmso_volume, daughter_column`. The protocol pauses midway through after the samples are placed and mixed in the middle plates for the user to inspect before resuming.
steps_raw: This protocol preps up to two 96 well plates with reagent and sample. The protocol takes a csv as input to determine which wells in the middle rack (see below) will be populated. The csv must include the header, with the following order in the header: `vial_id, parent_well, dmso_volume, daughter_column`. The protocol pauses midway through after the samples are placed and mixed in the middle plates for the user to inspect before resuming.
Read in CSV/Excel file that has a list of vial_ids, parent_wells, dmso_volumes, & daughter_columns.
Take specified dmso_volume from the DMSO in position A1 of 50 mL conical holder in slot 8.
Touch tips to remove droplets from pipette tip.
Add specified dmso_volume to parent_well position of CRO vial holder in slot 9.
Mix the vial at parent_well position of the CRO vial holder in slot 9 using half the dmso_volume added (same tip).
Touch tip to remove droplets from pipette tip.
Repeat steps 2-7 for all entries in the csv/excel file.
Pause to allow visual inspection of the vial.
Aspirate 240 uL of compound from parent_well position of CRO vial holder in slot 9.
Touch tips to remove droplets from the pipette tip.
Dispense 30 uL into all tubes of daughter_column and touch tips after each dispense.
Repeat steps 11-15 for all entries in the csv/excel file.
materials_raw: Opentrons P300 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
Opentrons P1000 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
